title: Teucrium pampaninii
intro: Teucrium pampaninii, also known as the Pampanini's germander, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to China. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Teucrium pampaninii include diterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Teucrium pampaninii is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It belongs to the genus Teucrium, which comprises approximately 300 species of evergreen or deciduous shrubs and perennial herbs. The specific epithet 'pampaninii' is often used to honor the Italian botanist Pietro Pampanini. Teucrium pampaninii is native to the Himalayan region and is characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and woody stems. This species is further categorized within the order Lamiales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants.
uses: Teucrium pampaninii is reported to have various medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
